title: Formula E reveals first calendar for GEN4 with lots of real race tracks
gist: Formula E's season 12 calendar for 2026-2027 expands to 21 races across 13 cities, now including major traditional circuits like Circuit of the Americas, Zandvoort, Brands Hatch, and Shanghai's F1 venue. The shift to traditional tracks reflects the GEN4 car's increased speed, which has outgrown city-center street circuit venues. The season introduces a new double-header weekend format with a 30-minute flat-out sprint on day one and a 45-minute energy-managed race on day two.

themes.theme: Formula E GEN4 Era Expansion and Format Evolution

insights.claim: Formula E's GEN4 season 12 calendar expands to 21 races across 13 cities, including traditional F1 venues Circuit of the Americas, Zandvoort, Brands Hatch, and a Shanghai F1 track—marking a departure from the series' original city-center-only positioning.
insights.speaker: Jonathan M. Gitlin
insights.so_what: The shift to established motorsport venues increases Formula E's exposure to traditional racing audiences and could attract broader broadcast and sponsorship deals.
insights.quote: a 21-race lineup across 13 cities that now includes several traditional race tracks

insights.claim: The GEN4 car is at times more powerful than a Formula 1 car, though heavier and with less downforce; its increased speed made several existing city-center Formula E venues too dangerous, forcing the switch to permanent racing circuits.
insights.speaker: Jonathan M. Gitlin
insights.so_what: Performance escalation is driving venue requirements upward, which may increase circuit hosting costs but also makes the series more competitive to traditional motorsport fans.
insights.quote: As speeds rise with the GEN4 car, we knew the sport would become too fast for some of its current venues.

insights.claim: Formula E will introduce a new double-header weekend format: day one features a 30-minute flat-out sprint race with high-downforce bodywork, while day two uses the traditional 45-minute energy-management format—giving each round two distinct competitive dimensions.
insights.speaker: Jonathan M. Gitlin
insights.so_what: Two-race weekends double the content creation opportunities per event, potentially increasing broadcaster value per round and attendee revenue.
insights.quote: On the first day, the cars will race for 30 minutes with high downforce bodywork... The second day will feature a more traditional 45-minute e-prix

insights.claim: COTA will be Formula E's seventh US location since 2015, reflecting sustained commercial interest in the US market, but the series will use NASCAR's shorter COTA configuration rather than the full F1 layout to avoid direct lap-time comparisons with F1.
insights.speaker: Jonathan M. Gitlin
insights.so_what: Choosing the shorter COTA layout signals that Formula E is managing perception versus F1 rather than competing directly, a branding strategy that preserves differentiation.
insights.quote: The race at COTA will use the shorter version of that circuit, as used by NASCAR for its visits, rather than the full F1 configuration. This spares the inevitable lap-time comparisons between the two series
